extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb won stage 5 bunch sprint after recovering from positioning deficit, passing VIVIANI and NIZOLO in final meters despite MALIA's dropped chain.
tactical_patterns: Alpecin-Fenix leadout for MALIA collapsed with dropped chain under 3km, exposing vulnerability of single-train sprint setups in technical finales.
NIZOLO sprinted isolated on opposite side of road into headwind for extended duration, nearly held off protected sprinters despite tactical disadvantage.
Two separate breakaway waves from same teams (Androni, Bardiani) at 68km and earlier suggests coordinated sponsor exposure strategy on guaranteed sprint stages.

rider_signals.SIVAKOV Pavel: negative: crashed with peloton nervousness on flat sprint stage, finished 13 minutes down, suggests positioning fragility
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: negative: abandoned race after crash inside 5km on flat stage, eliminated GC contender before mountains
rider_signals.MALIA Tim: negative: mechanical failure under 3km negated Alpecin-Fenix leadout investment, now 0-for-2 in sprint opportunities
rider_signals.NIZOLO Giacomo: positive: 11th career second place but leads points classification, demonstrates consistency in chaotic sprints despite poor positioning
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: recovered from poor positioning to win, shows exceptional acceleration when already committed late in sprint
